title: Penn Wharton: US Effective Tariff Rate at 10.3% — Highest Since 1943 Under Section 122
detail: Penn Wharton Budget Model updated its effective tariff rate (ETR) estimate to 10.3% through January 2026. With the replacement of IEEPA tariffs by Section 122 global tariffs, the projected bias-corrected ETR stands at 7.7%. China faces the highest rate at 33.9%; steel and aluminum face 41.1%. Total tariff revenue raised since Jan 2025: $209 billion. USMCA utilization surged to 85% for Canadian and Mexican imports as firms aggressively leveraged rules of origin exemptions.

title: High-Stakes USMCA Review Talks Begin Amid Trade War Tensions
detail: Formal USMCA review talks commenced on March 16, 2026, coinciding with the USTR's Section 301 investigations into Canada and Mexico. The review is complicated by the Supreme Court's February 20 IEEPA ruling, Section 122 tariff reimposition, and Canada's threat of retaliatory measures. Canada and Mexico are already in shallow recessions according to J.P. Morgan forecasts. The talks represent a critical juncture for North American trade architecture.
